title: Discrete-time hazard reformulation with stint-aware censoring
summary: This target is naturally a next-interval event hazard: probability of pitting next lap given no pit yet on the current stint.
rationale: This target is naturally a next-interval event hazard: probability of pitting next lap given no pit yet on the current stint. Discrete-time survival literature supports framing such problems as conditional hazards, and the newest F1 degradation paper validates one-step-ahead rolling-origin evaluation within race sessions.
implementation_hint: Create a hazard specialist trained only on laps where the current stint is ongoing, with baseline hazard terms over `LapInStint`, `LapsRemaining_Est`, and interactions with compound/weather regime. Validate with expanding or rolling origin splits by race-driver sequence, optionally with a fold gap, and ensemble its scores with the base classifier.
expected_effect: Often improves temporal consistency and reduces CV inflation from random folds, while explicitly modeling rising stop probability over a stint.
risk: If the current CV is random rather than chronological/grouped, scores may drop initially because leakage disappears; hazard framing can also underperform if censoring/reset logic is mishandled.
